coredata.dc:title: Biocompatible pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharide templated Pd nanoparticles for effectively colorimetric detection of glutathione
coredata.prism:publicationName: Colloids and Surfaces A: Physicochemical and Engineering Aspects
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Shukai
Zhao, Yu
Ji, Fangqin
Zheng, Ruixue
Ji, Xianbing
Liu, Zhiwei
Wang, Longgang
coredata.dc:description: Glutathione as a natural antioxidant can protect the human body from free radicals produced by oxidative stress. Therefore, it’s quite necessary to measure the concentration of glutathione to ensure our health. Herein, we prepared biocompatible pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharide templated palladium nanoparticles (Pdn-PCRP NPs, n = 150, 225, 300) by a green and facile synthetic method. The sizes of Pd150-PCRP NPs, Pd225-PCRP NPs, and Pd300-PCRP NPs were 2.10 ± 0.62 nm, 2.48 ± 0.62 nm, and 2.63 ± 0.62 nm, respectively. Pd150-PCRP NPs exhibited excellent biocompatibility based on the MTT assay. Moreover, Pd150-PCRP NPs oxidized the colorless peroxidase substrates 3,3′,5,5′ -tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to its corresponding blue product in the presence of hydrogen peroxide. More importantly, Pd150-PCRP NPs were used in the colorimetric detection of glutathione. The detection range of colorimetric detection with Pd150-PCRP NPs reached 2–300 μM and the detection limit was 1.65 μM. The recovery rate of glutathione in serum samples was as high as 103.60 %. Overall, these results demonstrated that Pd150-PCRP NPs had good biocompatibility, peroxidase-like activity and ability of glutathione detection. Pd150-PCRP NPs could serve as a safe and promising agent for sensitive detection of glutathione in future applications
coredata.dcterms:subject: Green synthesis
Nanoparticles
Polysaccharide
Glutathione
Detection

Catalytic activity kinetics and mechanism of Pd150-PCRP NPs

This research was funded by Natural Science Foundation of Hebei Province, China (B2017203229), Talent Training Programs of Hebei Province, China (A201901068, A201901069) and Scientific Research Key Projects of Education Department of Hebei Province, China (ZD2019024).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TEM images and statistical sizes of (a, d) Pd150-PCRP NPs; (b, e) Pd225-PCRP NPs; (c, f) Pd300-PCRP NPs.
XPS spectra of (a) element composition and (b) Pd valence state of Pd150-PCRP NPs.
(a) Hydrodynamic sizes and (b) zeta potentials of Pd150-PCRP NPs.
(a) Absorbance of the catalytic reaction of Pd150-PCRP NPs, Pd225-PCRP NPs and Pd300-PCRP NPs; catalytic activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs under different conditions: (b) pH and (c) temperature.
Catalytic kinetics of Pd150-PCRP NPs : the steady-state kinetics of Pd150-PCRP NPs on the substrate (a, c) TMB and (b, d) H2O2.
(a) Fluorescence intensity graph of different experimental groups; (b) fluorescence intensity value at 424 nm of different experimental groups.
Cell viability of PCRP and Pd150-PCRP NPs after incubation.
Pd150-PCRP NPs applied in glutathione detection:(a) linear graph of glutathione concentration; (b) the selective effect of other components on glutathione detection.

Comparison of K
m and V
max values of Pd150-PCRP NPs and other nanomaterials.
Detection range and detection limit of glutathione concentration based on Pd150-PCRP NPs and other nanomaterials.
Human serum glutathione content detection and recovery rate.

head.ce:title: Biocompatible pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharide templated Pd nanoparticles for effectively colorimetric detection of glutathione

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Metastable Materials Science and Technology, Key Laboratory of Applied Chemistry, Nano-biotechnology Key Lab of Hebei Province, Hebei Key Laboratory of Heavy Metal Deep-Remediation in Water and Resource Reuse, Qinhuangdao 066004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Metastable Materials Science and Technology, Key Laboratory of Applied Chemistry, Nano-biotechnology Key Lab of Hebei Province, Hebei Key Laboratory of Heavy Metal Deep-Remediation in Water and Resource Reuse

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Metastable Materials Science and Technology, Key Laboratory of Applied Chemistry, Nano-biotechnology Key Lab of Hebei Province, Hebei Key Laboratory of Heavy Metal Deep-Remediation in Water and Resource Reuse, Qinhuangdao, 066004, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Hebei University of Environmental Engineering, Qinhuangdao 066102, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Hebei University of Environmental Engineering, Qinhuangdao, 066102, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Glutathione as a natural antioxidant can protect the human body from free radicals produced by oxidative stress. Therefore, it’s quite necessary to measure the concentration of glutathione to ensure our health. Herein, we prepared biocompatible pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharide templated palladium nanoparticles (Pdn-PCRP NPs, n = 150, 225, 300) by a green and facile synthetic method. The sizes of Pd150-PCRP NPs, Pd225-PCRP NPs, and Pd300-PCRP NPs were 2.10 ± 0.62 nm, 2.48 ± 0.62 nm, and 2.63 ± 0.62 nm, respectively. Pd150-PCRP NPs exhibited excellent biocompatibility based on the MTT assay. Moreover, Pd150-PCRP NPs oxidized the colorless peroxidase substrates 3,3′,5,5′ -tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to its corresponding blue product in the presence of hydrogen peroxide. More importantly, Pd150-PCRP NPs were used in the colorimetric detection of glutathione. The detection range of colorimetric detection with Pd150-PCRP NPs reached 2–300 μM and the detection limit was 1.65 μM. The recovery rate of glutathione in serum samples was as high as 103.60 %. Overall, these results demonstrated that Pd150-PCRP NPs had good biocompatibility, peroxidase-like activity and ability of glutathione detection. Pd150-PCRP NPs could serve as a safe and promising agent for sensitive detection of glutathione in future applications

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Glutathione is an important antioxidant substance and is widely distributed in the organism. Glutathione extensively participates in various biological processes. Glutathione works in the body by capturing free radicals from oxidative stress to maintain the stability of the cellular environment [1]. Glutathione has a great influence on intracellular signal transmission, immune function regulation, cell detoxification, and metabolic process [2]. Thus, abnormal changes in the concentration of glutathione in the human body are relevant to many health problems, such as liver damage, heart problems, children’s white blood cell deficiency, neurodegenerative diseases [3], and cancer [4]. Therefore, it is quite necessary to develop a convenient method for detecting glutathione in human serum.

Nanozymes are defined as nanomaterials with enzyme-like properties [5]. Since the discovery of magnetic Fe3O4 nanoparticles (NPs) with peroxidase-like activities in 2007 [6], research on nanozymes has attracting more and more attention. A growing number of materials are found to possess a wide variety of enzyme-like activities [7]. Nanozymes are generally low-cost, easy to store, and easy production. In addition, the unique physicochemical properties of nanomaterials not only provide nanozymes with many functions but also provide many opportunities for logical design and various applications [8–10].

Colorimetry has attracted considerable attention due to its simple operation and fast feedback [11]. Peroxidase-like nanomaterial can catalyze the oxidation reaction of 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) in the presence of H2O2 to obtain blue oxidated TMB (oxTMB). And the color of oxTMB will gradually fade by adding glutathione and we can accurately judge the content of glutathione according to the depth of color measured by an ultraviolet-visible (UV–vis) spectrophotometer [12]. However, the detection range of glutathione by some colorimetric methods is quite narrow, which limits its wide application [13,14]. A case in point is that Streptococcus thermophilus is an important glutathione production strain, and the glutathione concentration in its production environment exceeds 30 μM [15]. So it is of great significance to develop a method with a larger detection range and low detection limit for the colorimetric detection of glutathione.

Pericarpium citri reticulatae (PCR), also known as Chenpi in Chinese, is the dry mature peel of Citrus reticulata Blanco. PCR as healthcare food and dietary supplement has been widely used in treating various diseases [16]. The effective active substances in PCR include flavonoids, total phenols, carotenoids, and polysaccharides [17]. Most researches focus on the role of flavonoids in PCR [18]. However, polysaccharides have important research value among the active substances of similar herbs [19–24], but the research on pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharides (PCRP) is very rare. Yang et al. extract PCRP with a weight-average molecular weight of 55.4 kDa by hydrothermal extraction at 65 °C for 120 min [25]. PCRP had a certain number of reducing groups, and these reducing groups can reduce Fe3+ to Fe2+, and the reducing ability of PCRP is concentration-dependent [26].

Herein, PCRP was used as a stabilizer and reducing agent to reduce Na2PdCl4 to prepare pericarpium citri reticulatae polysaccharide templated palladium nanoparticles (Pdn-PCRP NPs, n = 150, 225, 300), where the molar ratio of PCRP to Na2PdCl4 was 1:150, 1:225 and 1:300, respectively. Transmission electron microscope (TEM) characterization found that Pdn-PCRP NPs (n = 150, 225, 300) had small size and good dispersibility. And cellular experiments showed that Pd150-PCRP NPs had no cytotoxicity. Moreover, Pd150-PCRP NPs had good peroxidase-like activity and were applied to the detection of glutathione. Notably, the detection range reached 2–300 μM, and the recovery rate of detection serum samples was 103.60 %.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation and characterization of Pdn-PCRP NPs
ce:section.ce:para: The extracted and purified PCRP was dissolved in deionized water with a mass concentration of 1 mg/mL. The number-average molecular weight of PCRP was 74,930 Da. Then Na2PdCl4 solution (2 mM) was mixed with PCRP solution according to the molar ratio of PCRP to Na2PdCl4 for 1:150, 1:225, and 1:300, respectively. The reaction temperature was set at 60 °C for 24 h. After the reaction was completed, the solution was dialyzed with deionized water for another 24 h. The prepared solution was stored in the refrigerator at 4 °C.

In order to explore the effect of the feeding ratio of Na2PdCl4 and PCRP on the size of Pd NPs, TEM was used to measure the size of Pd NPs inside of Pdn-PCRP (n = 150, 225, 300). The feeding ratios of PCRR to Na2PdCl4 were 1:150, 1:225 and 1:300, respectively. The PCRP was used as the template, and the new formation of Pd nanoparticles should be loaded into the PCRP template. Fig. 1(a, b, c) showed that Pd NPs inside of Pdn-PCRP (n = 150, 225, 300) had good dispersibility. The sizes of Pd150-PCRP NPs, Pd225-PCRP NPs and Pd300-PCRP NPs were 2.10 ± 0.62 nm Fig. 1(d), 2.48 ± 0.62 nm Fig. 1(e), and 2.63 ± 0.62 nm Fig. 1(f), respectively. The size distribution of them was narrow. Compared with Pd NPs with the size of 4.8 ± 1.6 nm prepared by Kora and Rastogi using gum ghatti, the Pd NPs prepared by this method had a smaller size and narrower size distribution [27]. At the same time, as the feeding molar ratio of Na2PdCl4 to PCRP increased, the size of the generated Pd NPs increased slightly. Among them, Pd150-PCRP NPs had the smallest average size and the largest specific surface area, which indicated that the catalytic activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs could be the highest theoretically [28]. Thus, Pd150-PCRP NPs were used for further research. As shown in Fig. S2, FTIR results demonstrated there was no obvious structural change between PCRP and Pd150-PCRP NPs.

The element composition and Pd valence state of Pd150-PCRP NPs were explored through XPS. As shown in Fig. 2(a), the Pd150-PCRP NPs had three elemental compositions of C, O and Pd. And the XPS spectra of the Pd 3d3/2 and Pd 3d5/2 show the dual characteristic peaks at 336.3 eV and 341.1 eV in Fig. 2(b), respectively. This proved that the valence state of Pd NPs inside Pd150-PCRP NPs was also zero valence, which further proved the successful preparation of Pd150-PCRP NPs. Furthermore, the element sensitivity factor method was adopted to calculate the atomic percentage of C, O and Pd elements by XPS. C and O are derived from PCRP, and Pd is derived from the metal precursor Na2PdCl4. The atomic percentages of C, O and Pd are 66.30 %, 31.46 %, and 2.24 %, respectively.

DLS was a common method to explore the stability of nanoparticles in solution [29]. Since the detection application of Pd150-PCRP NPs was carried out in the solution, DLS was used to explore the stability of Pd150-PCRP NPs in solution under different conditions. As shown in Fig. 3(a), Pd150-PCRP NPs after 24 h had a similar hydrodynamic size with Pd150-PCRP NPs after 1 week, which indicated that Pd150-PCRP NPs stably existed in solution within 1 week. As shown in Fig. 3(b), the zeta potential of Pd150-PCRP NPs had a certain amount of negative charge. These negative charges may be derived from the carboxyl group on the PCRP.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Investigation of enzyme mimicking activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs
ce:section.ce:para: The peroxidase-like activity of the as-prepared Pd150-PCRP NPs was investigated through the catalytic oxidation of TMB in the presence of H2O2. As exhibited in Fig. 4 (a), the absorbance of Pd150-PCRP NPs+TMB+ H2O2 solution was the highest, demonstrating that Pd150-PCRP NPs had the highest catalytic activity in Pdn-PCRP NPs. Pd150-PCRP NP also had good peroxidase-like activity. The AHMT-Pd NPs prepared by Vinita et al. [30] also have peroxidase-like activity. As depicted in Fig. 4(b, c), Pd150-PCRP NPs had the best catalytic performance at pH= 4 and 30 °C. The optimal pH 4 was similar to reported noble metal nanozymes, which were determined by their own properties [33]. When the temperature was from 30 to 50 °C, the activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs exceeded 90 %. When the temperature was between 20 and 70 °C, the activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs was still higher than 50 % of the largest activity. Therefore, the following experiment was carried out at pH= 4 and temperature 30 °C.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Catalytic activity kinetics and mechanism of Pd150-PCRP NPs
ce:section.ce:para:
For the convenience of comparison,
Table 1 shows the K
m and V
max of different kinds of materials including Pd150-PCRP NPs, HRP and other reported mimetic enzymes. K
m and affinity are negatively correlated [31]. It could be seen that compared to other materials, Pd150-PCRP NPs had quite good TMB affinity due to a relatively small K
m (0.2 mM). Pd150-PCRP NPs also showed a higher V
max than that of MoS2-Pt74Ag26 NMs [35] This showed that Pd150-PCRP NPs had good performance on catalysis.

In order to explore the mechanism of the catalytic process, further research has been done. It has been proved by UV–vis spectra that H2O2 basically cannot react with TMB at room temperature without Pd150-PCRP NPs. However, the solution color changed significantly after adding Pd150-PCRP NPs, demonstrating that TMB was oxidized to blue oxTMB. It is speculated that the peroxidase-like activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs is ascribed to •OH produced by catalyzing the decomposition of H2O2. To explore whether there is •OH produced, terephthalic acid (TA), a fluorescent probe, was used to capture •OH to form 2-hydroxyterephthalic acid (TAOH) with a strong fluorescence at ca. 435 nm. As depicted in Fig. 6(a, b), when Pd150-PCRP NPs were added into TA and H2O2 solution, the fluorescence intensity curve of TAOH was significantly higher than the others. And the peak value of the group TA+H2O2 +Pd150-PCRP NPs was 684, which was about 5 times of the TA+H2O2 group without Pd150-PCRP NPs. The experimental results demonstrated that the catalytic mechanism of Pd150-PCRP NPs was mainly from the generation of •OH. Tao et al. [37] use the TA method to explore the similar characteristics of the gold NPs they prepared.

To explore the biocompatibility of Pd150-PCRP NPs, a standard MTT assay was conducted against HeLa cells as human cervical cancer cells. As shown in Fig. 7, the cell viability was still close to 100 % when the concentration of PCRP and Pd150-PCRP NPs was up to 60 μg/mL. This showed that they had no cytotoxicity at this concentration, which was an obvious advantage in the detection field. Compared with synthetic organic materials such as polypyrrole by Yang et al. [38], PCRP had better biocompatibility, and the introduction of Pd did not change the good biocompatibility of PCRP. This was beneficial to reduce the error caused by cell damage when Pd150-PCRP NPs were applied to biomolecule detection.

Pd150-PCRP NPs catalyzed the colorless TMB to produce blue oxTMB, and the reductive glutathione could reduce oxTMB to colorless TMB. Therefore, the glutathione concentration could have a linear relationship with the changed oxTMB concentration, which could be easily detected by colorimetry. Fig. 8(a) shows that a good linear correlation can be established between Δabsorbance (ΔA) of oxTMB at 652 nm and the concentration of glutathione in a range of 2–300 μM. It is found that the detection range was also much larger than that of other catalysts ( Table 2). The linear regression equation was ΔA= 1.0479 Cglutathione + 0.01402 (R2 =0.9946). Based on this, the limit of detection (LOD) was calculated to be 1.65 μM. Na+, K+, Fe3+, alanine (Ala), histidine (His), glucose and lactose were used to test the selectivity of this assay. The other components caused very weak absorbance changes as depicted in Fig. 8(b). This showed that the method was highly selective for the detection of glutathione.

Human serum samples were collected as real samples from healthy volunteers to study the use of Pd150-PCRP NPs to detect glutathione in practical applications. Colorimetry with Pd150-PCRP NPs was used to measure the glutathione concentration. The recovery rate of standard addition was calculated to be 103.6 % as shown in Table 3 which showed that this method was reliable for the detection of human serum glutathione.

In this work, a green method was used to successfully prepare Pdn-PCRP NPs (n = 150, 225, 300). These nanoparticles had an ultra-small size of 2–3 nm. Moreover, Pd150-PCRP NPs had good biocompatibility with HeLa cells. Notably, Pd150-PCRP NPs showed the peroxidase-like activity and catalyzed the oxidation reaction of TMB in presence of H2O2, the peroxidase-like activity of Pd150-PCRP NPs was mainly form •OH produced by the decomposition of H2O2 catalyzed by Pd150-PCRP NPs. Furthermore, the colorimetric method for glutathione detection using Pd150-PCRP NPs exhibited high selectivity and had a detection range of 2–300 μM and a detection limit at 1.65 μM. And glutathione in human serum was accurately detected by the established method based on Pd150-PCRP NPs. In summary, the prepared Pd150-PCRP NPs with ultra-small size and good biocompatibility have potential detection applications in clinical diagnosis.

Shukai Li: Investigation, Writing – original draft; Yu Zhao: Investigation, Methodology; Fangqin Ji: Writing – review & editing; Ruixue Zheng: Writing - review & editing; Xianbing Ji: Writing – review & editing; Zhiwei Liu: Methodology; Longgang Wang: Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was funded by Natural Science Foundation of Hebei Province, China (B2017203229), Talent Training Programs of Hebei Province, China (A201901068, A201901069) and Scientific Research Key Projects of Education Department of Hebei Province, China (ZD2019024).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.colsurfa.2022.129617.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:

.
